dashboard.label: Ping Monitor Setup
dashboard.description: Configure Ping Monitor settings - run this once after installation

div.p: Configure your Splunk index and sourcetype settings below, then click Save Configuration.

search.query: | inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | where _key="global" | eval status=if(configured="true", "✅ Configured", "⚠️ Not Configured") | table status, index, sourcetype, metrics_index | rename status as "Status", index as "Events Index", sourcetype as "Sourcetype", metrics_index as "Metrics Index" | append [| makeresults | eval Status="⚠️ Not Configured", "Events Index"="(not set)", "Sourcetype"="(not set)", "Metrics Index"="(not set)" | where NOT [| inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | where _key="global" | return $configured]]

div.p: Enter the index and sourcetype where your ping monitor data is being sent via HEC:

input.label: Metrics Index (leave blank if not using metrics mode)

div.p:
Events Index: The Splunk index where ping event data is stored
Sourcetype: Usually "ping_monitor" unless you customized it
Metrics Index: Only needed if you enabled metrics mode in your config

html.p: Click the search button below to save your configuration:

search.query: | makeresults | eval _key="global", index="$config_index$", sourcetype="$config_sourcetype$", metrics_index="$config_metrics_index$", configured="true" | outputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup append=false key_field=_key | eval result="✅ Configuration saved successfully! Navigate to Ping Monitor Overview to view your data." | table result | rename result as "Status"

html.p: After saving, this panel shows if data exists in your configured index:

search.query: | inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | where _key="global" | map search="index=$index$ sourcetype=$sourcetype$ | stats count" maxsearches=1 | eval count=if(isnull(count), 0, count)

ol.li: Make sure your ping monitor script is running and sending data to Splunk HEC
Navigate to Ping Monitor Overview to view your monitoring dashboard
Optionally enable the scheduled saved search "Update Health Lookup" for correlation features
